_instructions: Annotation of Shi, Toyonaga & Anderson (2023) Nano Letters — 'In Vivo RNA Delivery to Hematopoietic Stem and Progenitor Cells via Targeted Lipid Nanoparticles'. NOTE: The annotation guide referred to this paper as 'Bhatt et al. 2023' but the actual authors are Shi D, Toyonaga S, Anderson DG (MIT/Koch Institute). v2 final — updated with supplementary materials data (Table S1, Figures S3-S5, full Methods). Confidence: HIGH for formulation components (now directly confirmed from supp methods), HIGH for physicochemical data (Table S1), HIGH for experimental results.

paper_metadata.title: In Vivo RNA Delivery to Hematopoietic Stem and Progenitor Cells via Targeted Lipid Nanoparticles
paper_metadata.institution: MIT Koch Institute for Integrative Cancer Research / Department of Chemical Engineering
paper_metadata._name_correction: The annotation guide called this 'Bhatt et al. 2023' — that was an error. The correct citation is Shi, Toyonaga & Anderson 2023.

formulations.description: In vitro screening formulation with DLin-MC3-DMA ionizable lipid and DMG-PEG2000 (C14). Conjugated to anti-CD117 clone 2B8. Used only for EML cell line siCD45 knockdown (Fig 1b).

ionizable_lipid.name: DLin-MC3-DMA
ionizable_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'Ionizable lipids (MC3, Lipid 5, SM-102, and ALC-0315) were purchased from Cayman Chemicals'

helper_lipid.name: DSPC
helper_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'DSPC...purchased from Avanti Polar Lipids'

peg_lipid.name: DMG-PEG2000
peg_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'DSPC, DMG-PEG2000, DSG-PEG2000 were purchased from Avanti Polar Lipids'

maleimide_peg_lipid.name: DSPE-PEG2000-Maleimide
maleimide_peg_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'DSPE-PEG2000-Maleimide was purchased from NOF America'
maleimide_peg_lipid._note: This is a FIFTH lipid component incorporated during formulation for antibody conjugation. This is different from Breda (post-insertion).

molar_ratios.source: Supp Methods: 'ionizable lipid, DSPC, cholesterol, PEG-lipid, and functionalized maleimide PEG-lipid at a ratio of 50:10:38:1.5:0.5'

physicochemical._note: Table S1 only reports values for the optimized ALC-0315/C18 formulation, not individual lipid variants.

targeting.conjugation_method: TCEP reduction (5 eq, 37°C, 1h) → thiol-maleimide with DSPE-PEG2000-Maleimide in LNP → SEC purification (qEVoriginal, Izon Sciences) → Amicon ultrafiltration concentration
targeting._note_vs_breda: Different from Breda: (1) TCEP reduction instead of SATA thiolation, (2) maleimide PEG-lipid incorporated DURING formulation instead of POST-INSERTION, (3) Bio X Cell antibody instead of BioLegend

manufacturing.details: Ethanol phase (lipids) rapidly mixed with aqueous RNA in 10 mM citrate buffer pH 3.0 at 3:1 aqueous:ethanol ratio. Dialyzed ≥18h at 4°C in PBS + 10 mM EDTA using Slide-A-Lyzer 10K MWCO. Characterized by Malvern ZetaSizer (size, PDI) and RiboGreen (EE).
manufacturing.reference: Chen et al. JACS 2012 (ref 2 in supp)

formulations.description: In vitro screening. cKK-E12 synthesized in-house (Dong et al. PNAS 2014). Used only for Fig 1b.

ionizable_lipid.name: cKK-E12
ionizable_lipid.source_vendor: Synthesized in-house per Dong et al. 2014
ionizable_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'cKK-E12 was synthesized as previously described (ref 1)'

formulations.description: In vitro screening. Moderna's Lipid 5. Used only for Fig 1b.
In vitro screening. SM-102. Used only for Fig 1b.

ALC-0315 with DMG-PEG2000 (C14). Used in vitro (Fig 1b) and in vivo PEG-lipid comparison (Fig 1c-d). Baseline before PEG optimization. Selected for in vivo use due to FDA-approved safety profile of ALC-0315.

ionizable_lipid.name: ALC-0315
ionizable_lipid.source: Supp Methods + main text: 'we decided to use ALC-0315 as the ionizable lipid...because ALC-0315 has been FDA approved'

formulations.description: ALC-0315 with DPG-PEG2000 (C16). Intermediate in PEG alkyl chain length screen. Purchased from NOF America.

peg_lipid.name: DPG-PEG2000
peg_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'DPG-PEG2000...purchased from...NOF America'. Fig S3 x-axis: 'DPG-PEG (C16)'

formulations.description: *** OPTIMIZED FORMULATION *** ALC-0315 with DSG-PEG2000 (C18). Winner of PEG-lipid screen. Used for all key in vivo experiments: siCD45 dose-response, Cre mRNA editing, biodistribution. The C18 PEG-lipid has slower desorption from LNP surface → increased circulation time → improved bone marrow delivery.

peg_lipid.name: DSG-PEG2000
peg_lipid.source: Supp Methods: 'DSG-PEG2000 were purchased from Avanti Polar Lipids'. Fig S3 x-axis: 'DSG-PEG (C18)'

targeting.conjugation_method: TCEP partial reduction (5 eq, 37°C, 1h, gentle shaking) → excess TCEP removed by Zeba 7K MWCO desalting column → reduced Ab quantified by A280 NanoDrop → incubated with maleimide-LNPs 1h RT with end-over-end mixing → SEC purification (qEVoriginal columns, Izon Sciences, PBS mobile phase) → Amicon ultracentrifugation concentration → BCA protein quantification → stored 4°C

ligand_density_data.note: 1:20 is optimal; higher antibody (1:10) actually reduces uptake, possibly due to steric crowding

manufacturing.details: Ethanol phase (5 lipid components) rapidly mixed with aqueous RNA in 10 mM citrate buffer pH 3.0 at 3:1 aqueous:ethanol. Dialyzed ≥18h at 4°C in PBS + 10 mM EDTA (Slide-A-Lyzer 10K MWCO). For DiR-labeled uptake experiments: 0.5 mol% DiR included in ethanol phase.

formulations.description: Same optimized base LNP as F7 but conjugated to antagonistic clone ACK2. Poor performer in vivo. Used in biodistribution (Fig 3) and receptor screen (Fig 1f).
Negative control. Rat IgG2b isotype. Used across all key in vivo experiments.
No antibody. Untargeted control. Showed ~25% TdTomato at 1 mg/kg Cre mRNA — baseline HSPC transfection without targeting.

maleimide_peg_lipid.name: DSPE-PEG2000-Maleimide
maleimide_peg_lipid._note: Still contains maleimide PEG-lipid but no antibody conjugated

formulations.description: Alternative receptor screen. Ineffective for HSPC delivery (Fig 1f).
Alternative receptor screen. Ineffective for HSPC delivery (Fig 1f).
Alternative receptor screen. Ineffective for HSPC delivery (Fig 1f).

control_formulations: Isotype control LNP for each ionizable lipid

model_system.cell_type_or_organ: EML cells (ATCC CRL-11691, CD117+ hematopoietic progenitor cell line)
model_system.culture_conditions: IMDM + 1% Pen/Strep + 20% HI-FBS + 200 ng/µL murine SCF, 37°C 5% CO2

primary_outcome.metric: CD45 mRNA knockdown by qPCR (ddCt, B2m housekeeping, vs PBS control)
primary_outcome.source: Main text: 'All tested formulations showed very high level of silencing (50–80%) in vitro at a dose of 50 ng siRNA'

secondary_outcomes.note: LNPs conjugated to isotype control were not effective at transfecting EML cells

experiments.statistical_test: Multiple unpaired two-tailed t tests comparing isotype control of each group

ml_label.threshold_note: 50-80% knockdown across all 5 ionizable lipids — targeting is antibody-driven, not lipid-dependent

experiments.key_finding: CD117-targeting works with all tested ionizable lipids. The ionizable lipid identity is not the critical variable for targeted delivery.

payload.specific_cargo: siCD45 (DiR-labeled LNPs, 0.5 mol% DiR in ethanol phase)

primary_outcome.metric: % DiR+ cells in HSPCs by flow cytometry
primary_outcome.source: Main text: 'from around 30% uptake with conventional C14-PEG lipid to almost 90% uptake with C18-PEG lipid'

experiments.statistical_test: Two-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test

ml_label.efficacy_class: HIGH for C18; MEDIUM for C16; LOW for C14
ml_label.threshold_note: PEG-lipid alkyl chain length is the critical formulation parameter for bone marrow delivery. C18 >> C14.

experiments.key_finding: Longer PEG alkyl chains (slower desorption) dramatically improve bone marrow HSPC delivery. C18 gives ~3x uptake vs C14.

primary_outcome.source: Estimated from Fig S3 bar chart

experiments.statistical_test: One-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test

ml_label.efficacy_class: HIGH for C18; MEDIUM for C16 and C14
ml_label.threshold_note: Confirms PEG-lipid effect with functional mRNA delivery (not just uptake). C18 > C16 > C14.

experiments.key_finding: PEG-lipid alkyl chain effect confirmed with mRNA (Cre). C14→C18 improves functional editing from ~45% to ~70% at 0.3 mg/kg.

dosing.note: Dose conversion: 2/6/20 µg RNA = 0.1/0.3/1.0 mg/kg assuming ~20g mouse

primary_outcome.metric: DiR uptake in LSK (left panel) + CD45 MFI knockdown (right panel)
primary_outcome.source: Main text + supp methods: CD45 knockdown assessed by 'comparing the median fluorescence intensity of CD45 of treated LSK cells to the MFI of untreated control'

experiments.statistical_test: One-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test

ml_label.threshold_note: 40% protein-level CD45 knockdown at 1 mg/kg siRNA. Note: this is MFI-based protein knockdown, not mRNA-level.

secondary_outcomes.value: No significant depletion with any Ab-LNP vs PBS control
secondary_outcomes.note: All ns by one-way ANOVA with Dunnett's vs PBS. Important safety data.

secondary_outcomes.value: Free antibody mixed with unconjugated LNP showed minimal uptake
secondary_outcomes.note: Covalent conjugation is required — passive antibody adsorption is insufficient

secondary_outcomes.value: 2B8 = non-antagonistic; ACK2 = antagonistic. Both internalize in vitro (per ref 17) but only 2B8 works for LNP delivery. Reason unknown.
secondary_outcomes.note: Interestingly, in ADC context, 2B8-ADCs are also better at depleting HSCs than ACK2-ADCs (ref 17)

experiments.statistical_test: One-way ANOVA with Dunnett's multiple comparison test

ml_label.efficacy_class: HIGH for 2B8; LOW for all others
ml_label.threshold_note: Clone selection is critical. Same receptor, dramatically different LNP delivery outcomes.

experiments.key_finding: Only CD117 clone 2B8 works for HSPC-targeted LNP delivery. Other HSPC markers (CD44, CD49d, IL-6R) and the antagonistic CD117 clone ACK2 are all ineffective.

primary_outcome.metric: % TdTomato+ in HSPCs and LT-HSCs by flow cytometry
primary_outcome.source: Main text: '0.3 mg/kg...about 75% TdTomato+ cells in both HSPC and LT-HSC...20 µg of mRNA, almost all (~90%)...Unconjugated LNPs at a dose of 1 mg/kg...about 25% TdTomato'

experiments.statistical_test: Two-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test
experiments.n_per_group: n=4 mice (1 mg/kg Ab-LNP), n=3 mice (all other groups)

ml_label.threshold_note: ~90% HSPC and LT-HSC editing at 1 mg/kg. ~75% at 0.3 mg/kg. Both exceed 30% threshold. THE key number for this paper.

experiments.key_finding: Single IV injection edits ~90% of both HSPCs and LT-HSCs. Untargeted LNPs still achieve ~25% — indicating some inherent bone marrow tropism with C18-PEG formulation.

model_system.cell_type_or_organ: Peripheral blood (myeloid CD11b+, B cells B220+, T cells CD3e+, erythrocytes TER-119+)
model_system.blood_collection: Submandibular bleed into EDTA-coated BD Microtainer

dosing.note: Same mice as E5, tracked longitudinally

primary_outcome.metric: % TdTomato+ in peripheral blood populations over time

values.4wk_CD4_T_cells: > CD8 T cells at each timepoint

ml_label.threshold_note: Near-complete multilineage reconstitution: 90% myeloid, 70% B, 50% T, ~100% erythrocytes at 14wk. Proves stem cell-level editing with maintained stemness.

experiments.key_finding: Edited HSPCs produce corrected progeny across all lineages. ~100% erythrocyte editing at 14 weeks is the key therapeutic metric for hemoglobinopathies.

model_system.cell_type_or_organ: Whole-body organ panel: lung, heart, liver, spleen, intestines, femur/tibia, skin

dosing.imaging: IVIS Spectrum (PerkinElmer) after IP D-luciferin (130 µL of 30 mg/mL, 10 min prior to sacrifice)

values.CD117_2B8: Lung highest; bone marrow, intestines, skin significantly above isotype; also spleen/liver
values.CD117_ACK2: Some lung signal (lower than 2B8); no significant difference from isotype elsewhere

secondary_outcomes.value: Highest signal with 2B8 clone is in lung — unexpected since CD117 is lowly expressed in lung
secondary_outcomes.note: Paper suggests nonspecific antibody binding. CD117 is on intestinal Cajal cells and skin melanocytes, explaining those signals. Lung signal remains unexplained.

experiments.statistical_test: Two-way ANOVA with Dunnett's multiple comparison test vs isotype

ml_label.threshold_note: Bone marrow delivery confirmed but significant off-target lung signal. Lower dose (0.3 mg/kg) than Cre experiments.

primary_outcome.metric: % DiR+ in BM HSPCs at varying antibody:maleimide ratios
primary_outcome.source: Estimated from Fig S4 bar chart

experiments.statistical_test: One-way ANOVA with Tukey's multiple comparison test

ml_label.threshold_note: Optimal ratio is 1:20 antibody:maleimide. Too much antibody (1:10) reduces uptake — possible steric crowding or aggregation.

experiments.key_finding: Antibody density has an optimum — not monotonically increasing. Critical manufacturing parameter.

label_for_ml.primary_metric_used: % TdTomato+ in LT-HSCs (LSK CD34- CD135-) by flow cytometry

different_conjugation_chemistry.shi: TCEP reduction → thiol-maleimide with DSPE-PEG-Mal incorporated DURING formulation as 5th lipid component (0.5 mol%). SEC + ultrafiltration purification.
different_conjugation_chemistry.breda: SATA thiolation → thiol-maleimide with DSPE-PEG-Mal POST-INSERTED into preformed LNPs. Sepharose CL-4B purification.
different_conjugation_chemistry.implication: Shi's approach is simpler (fewer steps) but maleimide may be partially hydrolyzed during formulation at pH 3. Breda's post-insertion may give better control over antibody orientation.

different_peg_lipid.breda: Standard short-chain PEG (likely DMG-PEG2000 or ALC-0159, C14)

cross_references_to_breda_2023.different_antibody_source: Bio X Cell (Shi) vs BioLegend (Breda) — same clone 2B8 but different vendors

different_lthsc_definition.note: Overlapping but not identical populations. Important caveat for ML cross-paper comparisons.

efficacy_comparison.breda: 55% LT-HSC at ~0.25 mg/kg (5 µg in ~20g mouse)
efficacy_comparison.note: Not directly comparable: different doses, different LT-HSC definitions, different ionizable lipids, different PEG-lipids. Shi's C18-PEG likely contributes significantly to higher efficacy.

key_updates_from_supp: Exact PEG-lipid identities: DMG-PEG2000 (C14), DPG-PEG2000 (C16), DSG-PEG2000 (C18)
Table S1 physicochemical data: unconjugated 71.24 nm/0.06 PDI/90% EE; conjugated 91.04 nm/0.14 PDI/92% EE
Full conjugation protocol: TCEP reduction, not SATA
Antibody vendor: Bio X Cell (not BioLegend)
Mouse strain confirmed: C57BL/6 female 6-10wk; Ai14 bred in-house both sexes
Ligand density optimization data (Fig S4): optimal 1:20 Ab:maleimide ratio
Fig S3 Cre mRNA PEG-lipid comparison: C14 ~45%, C16 ~60%, C18 ~70% at 0.3 mg/kg
Fig S5 safety: no HSPC depletion with any Ab-LNP
Added experiments E2b (Fig S3) and E8 (Fig S4)
annotation_notes.naming_correction: Paper is Shi, Toyonaga & Anderson 2023, NOT 'Bhatt et al. 2023' as listed in annotation guide.
